domain:
In the context of a theater organization, various plays are performed in one or more theaters, each identified by its own location, for example "The Northwest Theater". A play may have multiple scheduled sessions, and each session occurs on a scheduled date. Plays are identified by a unique name and is either a musical or an opera. Furthermore, each play may take place during the day or at night.

Each play is written by one or more authors, who are uniquely identified by name. Plays involve participants each also identified by name, and the same participant may act in multiple plays. For each play session, the starting time and an ending time is recorded, and participants can act in one or more sessions. Both authors and participant can be the same person, who may write a play and participate in its performance.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Author in the relationship with Play?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a play can exist without any associated author.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a play must have at least one associated author.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Play can exist without Author.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Play must have at least one Author.
questions.selected: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Play must have at least one Author.

questions.question:
Should the relationship between Play and Participant be modeled as a composition or as an association?

For example:
1. Use composition, Participant would depend on Play for its existence.
2. Use association, Participant could exist independently of Play, and can also be linked to multiple plays.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Composition is needed because class Participant depends on Play.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Association is sufficient because class Participant exists independently of Play.
questions.selected: Option 2: Association is sufficient because class Participant exists independently of Play.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Play in the relationship with Participant?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a participant can exist without any associated play.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a participant must have at least one associated play.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Participant can exist without Play.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Participant must have at least one Play.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Participant can exist without Play.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Participant in the relationship with Play?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a play can exist without any associated participant.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a play must have at least one associated participant.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Play can exist without Participant.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Play must have at least one Participant.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Play can exist without Participant.

questions.question:
What is the maximum cardinality of Participant in the relationship with Play?

For example:
1. Maximum cardinality of one, a play is associated with exactly one participant.
2. Maximum cardinality of many, a play can be associated with multiple participants.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each play links to one participant.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each play can link to multiple participants.
questions.selected: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each play can link to multiple participants.
questions.llm_raw_response:
Option 2: Many as maximum cardinality, each play can link to multiple participants.

This option is better because the domain description states that plays involve participants and that the same participant may act in multiple plays, indicating that a play can have multiple participants.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Play in the relationship with Session?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a session can exist without any associated play.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a session must have at least one associated play.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Session can exist without Play.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,Session must have at least one Play.
questions.selected: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,Session must have at least one Play.

questions.question:
Should location be modeled as a separate class associated with Theater, or remain as a simple representation?

For example:
1. Location is a separate class with its own attributes. This allows users to query and filter by individual location properties.
2. Location is modeled as a simple representation in Theater. This is suitable when location details are not needed.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The Location is a separate class with its own attributes.
questions.option_2: Option 2: The location is a simple representation in Theater.
questions.selected: Option 2: The location is a simple representation in Theater.

questions.question:
Should the location in theater be modeled as a an attribute which allows free text input, or as an enumeration with predefined literals?

For example:
1. Use location as string attribute in theater, which allows any text value.
2. Use location as an enumeration which uses predefined values in literals: the northwest theater.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: String attribute that allows any text value for location.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Enumeration location with predefined values: literal 1, literal 2 and literal 3.
questions.selected: Option 1: String attribute that allows any text value for location.

questions.question:
Should musical and opera be represented as separate subclasses of Play, or as instances of Play distinguished by an enumeration attribute?
Consider whether these types have distinct attributes or behavior.

For example:
1. Musical and Opera are modeled as separate subclasses of Play. Each subclass can have its own specific attributes and methods.
2. A single Play class with a Type enumeration attribute with literals: musical or opera. All types share the same structure.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Use inheritance, create separate subclasses for musical and opera.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Use enumeration Play type with with literals: musical or opera.
questions.selected: Option 2: Use enumeration Play type with with literals: musical or opera.

questions.question:
Should Session be modeled as an association class linking Play and Theater, or as a regular class with two separate associations?

For example:
1. Association class: Session exists only as part of the relationship between Play and Theater. A session cannot exist without the link between Play and Theater.
2. Regular class: Session is a class that has links to Play and Theater. In this case multiple sessions can exist for every Play and Theater.


Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Association class, Session as association class linking Play and Theater.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Regular class, Session as separate class with two associations.
questions.selected: Option 1: Association class, Session as association class linking Play and Theater.

questions.question:
Should Person be modeled as an abstract class or as a concrete class?
Consider whether Person can be instantiated directly, or only through its subclasses (Author or Participant).

For example:
1. Concrete class  Person ithat can be instantiated. Class people can exist independently.
2. Abstract class  Person that cannot be instantiated. Every person must be a specific type author or participant.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Concrete class Person that can be instantiated directly.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Abstract class Person, where only the subclasses can be instantiated.
questions.selected: Option 2: Abstract class Person, where only the subclasses can be instantiated.
